title: tephroseris lanceolata subsp. arvernensis
intro: Tephroseris lanceolata subsp. arvernensis, also known as Alpine Saw-wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may contribute to its traditional uses in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Alpine Saw-wort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to fully explore its therapeutic potential and optimal preparation methods.
